item.title: Question
item.content:encoded: it says SCP-1879 infection though there no other mention of it to my knowledge, so what is the infection?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love this one so much! Poor Mrs. Yeats tho.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
“I want your soul.”

“What now?”

“Your Aretha Franklin.”

“Ah… I see.”

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Maybe one thing they could have done was bargain with him for details. He's gotta sell stuff? He needs to meet quota's? Make deals with the foundation for info by using D-Class for transactions.
I mean, I doubt it would work, he's probably refuse or find some kind of loophole to screw with them, but it's a possibility.

Also is the dog alright? What happened to the dog!?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is amazing, and lord bung just did a vid inspired on it:

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=8L0wQgPa20o

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the part about an anomalous salesman who will never, ever leave you alone until you buy something. But the "fae who ask for a weird price" part wasn't interesting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So, essentially Devil's Deal but nonnegotiable and the items are all tangible 'n' stuff. And Random Door Salesman too.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The thought of Jim Carrey running through little pocket pathways and trying to trade people various items for concepts and other anomalous prices just to move up in its strange line of employment…

It tickles me profusely.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Within one to three seconds of this statement, approximately two thousand (2,000) coins of assorted origin manifested within the pockets, epidermis, and digestive tract of all subjects at the scene excluding SCP-1879-2.

Jesus Christ, that's brutal. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Keter SCP with a bit of humor inside. I liked it.
I felt a sentence "No attempt of preventing its revisit permanently after a deal has been declined has been successful so far" would be fitting, though admittedly unnecessary. - may help improve keter-istic mood of it. I mean, a dangerous deal that actually gets more and more dangerous (because he keeps increasing the price) but has to be accepted at some point would be real scary.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I mostly like the Log with the Dog!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It shows the format being used for this documentation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I do quite like this article, but is there any particular reason the first retrieval log is blank? I'm hesitant to correct it myself in case it's intentional for whatever reason.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think this SCP is a great example of a Keter-SCP that doesn't need to be "world-devastating" or anything larger like with other Keters like 231 or 196. +1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: In this case, yes, this is the best option. You can't perfectly and effectively contain every single anomaly in the world; life is never that convenient. With this, the best you can really do is limit exposure as much as possible and take away what it gives people quickly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The part where I read it as coins coming out of the pockets, but I got it now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So, the best choice is to leave the guy who's handing out thermonuclear weapons free? Good idea.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Okay, best case scenario here is that he manifests a door for himself and walks out. Worst case is that he makes something that either a) compromises or damages the Foundation immensely, or b) exposes a large amount of civilians to anomalous behavior.

Now, if the answer to this procedure is "he creates something to escape(up to the reader's imagination)", then he is what I'd call an SCP-Sue: uncontainable/scary not because of reasons, but just because. And that's lame as heck.

…Scary? Now, if this guy was a malicious uncontrollable beast of rage and evil, I'd be inclined to agree with you.

However, he is not.

He is a salesman.

He doesn't necessarily want to hurt people, he just wants to make his goddamn sale. Furthermore, there is quite a large precedent for having uncontained skips.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes, hello. It gave them coins, it did not take them. Which part are you not understanding?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The stinger here is how he gets away. Here's a simple-ish procedure to attempt containment that I cooked up while reading the report:

1. If there's no containment facilities in Washington, build one.
2. Build a room with only one door out and one closet. Lock one or more D-Class inside.
3. Keep intercepting the salesman until he comes a-knocking from the closet in the facility.
4. Seal the door(through some mechanism activated externally; like, say, dropping molten iron until it turns the whole hole into an iron wall) and have the D-classes answer the door.
5. Pull the guy out and seal the door back as well.
6. Congratulations: it is contained with no way out! So are the D-classes, but hey! They're D-classes.

Now, if the answer to this procedure is "he creates something to escape(up to the reader's imagination)", then he is what I'd call an SCP-Sue: uncontainable/scary not because of reasons, but just because. And that's lame as heck.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Within one to three seconds of this statement, approximately two thousand (2,000) coins of assorted origin manifested within the pockets, epidermis, and digestive tract of all subjects at the scene excluding SCP-1879-2.

Um, hi?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's just something amusing to think about; plus, if you want to be lame as heck about this, you can always imagine him manifesting something powerful enough to break out of containment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
1. That was put in there to show the range of what the entity could produce before expanding on it.
  2. They definitely did not have that much money in their pockets. It fact, it was more money in their… well, "pockets."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Interesting, but a question remains: why didn't the Foundation even try it? All the items this guy sells are mundane so far, there's no background for thinking he could have such a thing as a "Dimensional Doorway". Heck, not even SCPs came from him, so what setup we have for such a conclusion?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Good concept, well executed, fucking loved the "soul" test. Upvote.

Some things, though:

1) Is it just me, or that first footnote is totally unnecessary? It doesn't add more than the logs right below, and they actually give more information. It's not an exhaustive list, so it could contain anything else, and the ones in aren't even that notable.

2) I think I missed the twisted logic on that last test. "No money out of your pocket" adn then… money out of pockets? I know we deal with anomalies, but inner logic is something my brain hungers for.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"…the subject later discovered two (2) of her Aretha Franklin albums (Lady Soul and Almighty Fire) to be missing."

Motherfucker.

I'd go on, but my brain is full of enjoyment and the words won't come out. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Can't help but feel sorry for the salesman. Granted, he does seem to consider people "beneath him", but if he doesn't do it, he's stuck here for another century. Hell, the Foundation could direct him to the D-class facilities and he'd be gone in no time.
Of course, that's not the point, the point is to contain him, but still.
That and you don't want a bunch of convicted criminals running around with Glocks, nukes, etcetra.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love it when people understand my skips well enough to do shit like this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Let's say the Foundation makes a special room and manages to lure the salesman in, slamming a massive armored plate over the only door as soon as he enters. YAY, containment! Right? Not so fast.

Salesman speaking to cameras: Hello again! Thanks to your enthusiastic interest in our products, my manager has authorized me to make you a special, limited-edition, today-only offer! As our gift to you, free of charge, no purchase neccessary, please have this complimentary Dimensional Doorway (patent pending). Let me demonstrate it's operation.

Salesman pulls a looney tunes style portable hole out of his pocket, sticks it on the wall, and steps through. Upon investigation, the portal links to 682's home dimension and can't be closed or destroyed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It comes out of any door. If one is locked, it comes through a new one. If all the doors are locked, it kicks down the fucking door and sells you its shit.

It doesn't need to end the world to be Keter.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't know if it should be Keter - after you identify the doors it comes out of, you could lock the whole thing up and monitor for future instances. After all, it can't necessarily end the world…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's variable. If he thinks he can still pull off a sale at that moment when he's bloody and beaten, he will still damn well try. It's only when the person hurts him enough in a way that either is lethal or shows that they won't listen to him at all that he retreats to try again later.

Of course, the Foundation isn't quite willing to find out the extent of this given the results of the last log. It'd be easier to appease the thing and keep the price at a minimum than lose personnel. I'm sorry if that's a cop-out ._.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Just out of interest, you say in the description that if it is "sufficiently threatened or wounded" it would retreat, but then there are no examples of it being wounded. You'd think that with the Keter classification and obvious danger of this skip they would have just shot him in the head?
I know it's not the DDD Foundation but still.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this a lot. It has personality, and it's mysterious and Keter-y without being all "HOMG death and destruction."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Moar anomalous salesmen/suppliers! Another guy who gives you a specific item for a "literal genie"-price!… Oi… no vote, because I liked it, but these types of SCP are overdone, and combining the two didn't help me forget the half dozen or so others on the site, and I didn't feel that it stood out enough from the crowd…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The logs got better toward the end, but for the most part I was underwhelmed by the 'prices'. I've seen almost all of those before; they're neither original nor terribly shocking. I will say that I'm intrigued by the idea of a Keter that isn't always inimical to human life. No vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nukes in the right hands are safe class. Nukes anomalously appearing in the hands of random civilians of unknown moral character is not. But either way, this thing isn't handing out nukes all over the place either, so the issue is kind of moot.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Indeed, we have SCP-1286, which is an anomalous atomic bomb that might explode at any moment and there's not a whole lot we can do about it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If it was an anomalous nuke that could set itself off (which I think we have), it might be Keter. Otherwise, I think it says on the Safe-class page that nuclear weapons are examples of Safe-class objects.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The previous version of the Keter page listed a nuke that is armed and could go off as an example of a Keter.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It totally is. Nukes are more dangerous than the overwhelming majority of SCPs. Handing them out is giving random people the ability to level cities. And it's almost impossible to track.

(1) If nukes were an SCP, they'd be Safe class. Nukes actually are the ur-example of a dangerous Safe-class.

(2) The Foundation isn't trying to contain people leveling cities. Technically leveling cities is perfectly okay by them, although they might object in practice if discovering a mundane plot like that.

EDIT: This is definitely Keter, though, by potential and uncontainability alone.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Liked it in chat, like it here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It totally is. Nukes are more dangerous than the overwhelming majority of SCPs. Handing them out is giving random people the ability to level cities. And it's almost impossible to track.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I hear the voice of Jason Lee for 1879-1 for some reason. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't think one of the qualifiers for Keter class is "violating the Nuclear Non-Proliferation Treaty". Just sayin'.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yep, but even then it targets 1 person at a time. There are plenty of serial killer Euclids, but the difference between them and SCP-106 is scale and magnitude of what they are doing. (As well as that whole actively hostile thing.)

With that said, I just remembered it offered a nuclear explosive, so Keter wouldn't be unwarranted given that it's passing out weapons on that level.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not sure where you're getting the "malevolent towards humans" thing. Has it killed people? Yes. People it didn't like? Yes, purely because they were preventing him from completing his job. Has he killed people as part of a "price?" Yes, but not maliciously; rather, that person's death is a result of him needing something to fill an unknown condition for his quota.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It needs human life in general to continue. Not any individual one. It's a serial killer that isn't always fatal.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's not inimmicable to human life. In fact it very much needs human life to continue.

There are already uncontainable near godly beings rated as euclid and safe because they dont just go around blasting stuff.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, this isn't working as much for me. The last log takes it too far into Good Omens territory for me to like it on its own merits.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The last log is the one that strikes you as silly?

Hm.

Anyways, point taken, changing class now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This doesn't do it for me. The last log is kinda silly. Plus, this is totally Keter. It's uncontainable, omnipotent in its domain, and hostile to the Foundation and innocent bystanders.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fixed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Gender?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fixed

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't think it describes anywhere what 1879-2 looks like.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
traveling salesman, just needs a minute of your time ma'am.

sticks his foot in the door.

"I just need. A minute."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Salesman of Death?

Me like.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ehhh… it's the same as the telemarketer-J, but not as funny.
